domain: Maasai shuka red-and-black checker garment (visually-distinctive non-textual identity marker; robust to deformation; sub-variations encode age/status/occasion; frozen canonical template)
specific_mechanism: Style-only visual identity signal carried by frozen pattern + color; survives partial occlusion; categorical sub-variation; no semantic payload beyond identity.
llm_application: SHUKA-STYLE-MARK: an output identity signal encoded ENTIRELY in stylometric features (sentence length distribution targeting a fixed bimodal profile, function-word frequency conformance, punctuation cadence — measured/regularized in a non-content null-space) with NO token-distribution bias, NO hidden tokens, and NO content modification. Detectable post-hoc by a stylometric classifier; per-checkpoint sub-variations (red-shuka vs blue-shuka) encoded via small shifts in the stylometric target profile. Different from token-frequency watermarks and from latent-direction fingerprints — the signal lives in style statistics independent of content.
novelty_claim: MarkLLM / KGW / SemStamp / Robust Watermarking / Semantically Conditioned Watermarks all bias token distribution. HuRef leverages parameter-vector direction invariants (not output style). Domain-specific watermarks use prompt-conditioned token biases. SHUKA-STYLE-MARK differs by using STYLOMETRIC FEATURES ONLY (sentence-length distribution, function-word frequency, punctuation cadence) as the watermark carrier, with NO token-distribution bias — the watermark is in the writing rhythm not the word choice.
